<!-- generated by sofar — do not hand-edit; truth lives in events.jsonl -->

# Decisions: cli-ui

- 2026-07-11T11:39:02.609Z — chose **Styled static output + stderr spinners at the emit()/renderer boundary, semantic ANSI-16 color law (Codex styles.md model: green=success, red=error, cyan=identifiers, dim=secondary; no hex for text, no bg detection), vendored cli-spinners frame sets per use case, degradation ladder NO_COLOR > --no-color > FORCE_COLOR > TTY, animation only when isTTY && !CI; agent-facing surfaces (renderStatus digest, hook stdout, NDJSON, mcp stdio) guaranteed byte-plain.** over (a) a TUI framework (Ink/OpenTUI/ratatui class — React+Yoga event loop for one-shot print-and-exit commands, heavy dep-law violation); (b) truecolor themes with terminal background detection (OSC queries are flaky in print-and-exit processes: pipes, CI, tmux); (c) chalk/ora comfort stack (chalk lacks NO_COLOR support entirely; ora is 17 packages for a spinner). because Research across Claude Code, Gemini CLI, opencode, Codex CLI, Kiro + Node ecosystem (2026-07-11, three-agent sweep): every fancy animation (Claude's eased ✳ pulse, Codex shimmer, opencode Knight Rider) requires a persistent render loop — wrong shape for sofar's one-shot commands; Codex's own non-interactive mode and the Vite/Astro/Vitest precedent (picocolors or vendored forks) converge on exactly this design; ANSI-16 semantic colors survive every terminal theme without dark/light detection.
- 2026-07-11T11:52:13.632Z — chose **Zero-dependency hand-rolled UI kernel in packages/engine/src/cli/ui/: vendor picocolors' color-detection + formatter-nesting logic (~40 lines), cli-spinners frame data as typed constants, and the is-unicode-supported gate (~15 lines) — all MIT, attributed in source comments.** over Adding picocolors as a dependency (1 pkg, 32 KB, zero transitive — the Vite/Next.js route); chalk/ora comfort stack (already rejected in D1: chalk lacks NO_COLOR, ora is 17 packages); Node's util.styleText (needs >=20.12, repo floor is Node 18). because Keeps the dependency ledger untouched entirely (repo law treats every new dep as a Decision-worthy event; zero-dep needs no ongoing supply-chain trust); Vitest (tinyrainbow) and Astro (piccolore) set the precedent of vendoring picocolors-class logic; ~130 lines total sits comfortably inside this repo's test discipline; and we control NO_COLOR > FORCE_COLOR precedence spec-compliantly, which ansis and util.styleText get wrong.
